name: Tver Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: courage and the historical colour of Tver
wealth and the gold of the princely throne

description: A red field flanked by two narrow yellow vertical stripes at the hoist and fly; the coat of arms of Tver Oblast (a gold throne bearing a crown) is centred on the red panel.
history: The flag of Tver Oblast was adopted on 28 November 1996. The arms with the throne and crown have represented the medieval Principality of Tver in Russian heraldry since the 16th century.

features.symbolism: a broad red field bordered hoist and fly by narrow gold stripes, drawn from the historic arms of the Tver principality.
a gold princely throne with a crown on a green cushion, the historic arms of Tver dating to the 16th century.
